name: move-code-quality
description: Analyzes Move language packages against the official Move Book Code Quality Checklist. Use this skill when reviewing Move code, checking Move 2024 Edition compliance, or analyzing Move packages for best practices. Activates automatically when working with .move files or Move.toml manifests.
capabilities: Scans Move projects for Move.toml and .move files
Reviews function signatures and parameter ordering
Validates module syntax, imports, and constants naming
Evaluates modern Move macros and syntax patterns
author: davila7
securitySummary: This is a pure prompt-based skill containing only documentation and AI instructions. No executable code, file system access, network calls, or command execution exists in the skill definition. All 139 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES caused by the scanner misinterpreting Move code examples, error constants, and documentation links.
valueStatement: Move code reviews require deep knowledge of the Move Book Code Quality Checklist. This skill provides automated analysis against 50 rules for Move 2024 Edition best practices including code organization, function signatures, testing patterns, and modern syntax.
limitations: Does not execute or compile Move code

useCases.title: Code Review Before Deploy
useCases.description: Review Move packages against official checklist before mainnet deployment

useCases.title: Blockchain Security Analysis
useCases.description: Audit smart contract code for quality issues and best practice violations

useCases.title: Learn Modern Move Patterns
useCases.description: Get feedback on code while learning Move 2024 Edition standards

promptTemplates.title: Full Package Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my Move package for code quality issues. Check Move.toml configuration, all .move files, and test modules against the Move Book checklist.

promptTemplates.title: Specific File Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check this Move file for quality issues. Focus on function signatures, parameter ordering, and modern syntax usage.

promptTemplates.title: Manifest Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my Move.toml file. Check edition specification, dependencies, and named address configuration.

promptTemplates.title: Testing Best Practices
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my Move tests. Check test attributes, naming conventions, assertions, and cleanup patterns.

bestPractices: Run this skill before committing Move code to catch quality issues early
Focus on critical issues first - Move 2024 Edition is required for modern features
Use the provided examples to understand both problematic and correct patterns
antiPatterns: Skipping this review before mainnet deployment
Ignoring parameter ordering guidelines - they enable proper composability
Using outdated explicit framework dependencies in Move.toml

faq.question: Which Move editions are supported?
faq.answer: Only Move 2024 Edition (2024.beta or 2024) is supported. All checklist features require this edition.

faq.question: Does this skill compile my code?
faq.answer: No. This skill analyzes code structure and patterns. Use the Move compiler for syntax checking.

faq.question: Can this skill fix issues automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides detailed feedback with code examples but does not modify your files.

faq.question: Is my code data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only reads files you explicitly request to analyze. No code leaves your environment.

faq.question: How does this compare to a Move linter?
faq.answer: This skill checks 50 best practice rules including naming, structure, and composability that linters may miss.

faq.question: What blockchain frameworks work with this skill?
faq.answer: Primary focus is on Sui Move patterns. The checklist aligns with Move Book standards for all Move chains.
